seo.title: Building Voice Agents with LlamaIndex + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build voice agents with LlamaIndex + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building Voice Agents with LlamaIndex enables real-time conversational AI by connecting speech-to-text pipelines to indexed knowledge bases for grounded LLM responses. Guardrails are essential due to the high-stakes, low-latency nature of voice interactions where irrelevant retrievals, hallucinations, or delays can break user trust and conversation flow. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure response quality, safety, and performance in this critical pipeline.

architectureOverview.heading: Voice Agents Architecture with LlamaIndex
architectureOverview.description:
Voice Agents process audio input through a real-time speech-to-text (STT) service like Deepgram or Whisper Live, converting speech to text with sub-second latency. The transcribed query is then routed to a LlamaIndex query engine, which retrieves relevant context from vector/index stores (e.g., FAISS, Pinecone) and generates a grounded LLM response. The response is verified for quality and safety before text-to-speech (TTS) synthesis via services like ElevenLabs or Cartesia, completing the loop in under 500ms.

LlamaIndex handles retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) by chunking documents, embedding them, and enabling semantic search. Common setups use VectorStoreIndex for fast retrieval and OpenAI/Groq LLMs for reasoning. Vex integrates at the query engine to catch failures before TTS, preserving low-latency guarantees.

The pipeline demands strict latency budgets: STT (<200ms), retrieval+LLM (<200ms), verification (<50ms), TTS (<100ms). Stateless conversation history via LlamaIndex chat engines maintains coherence across turns.

risks.name: Retrieved context irrelevant to query
risks.description: LlamaIndex retrieves semantically similar but topically irrelevant chunks, leading to hallucinated or off-topic voice responses.
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic relevance checks between query and retrieved context before LLM generation.

risks.name: Synthesizer hallucinating beyond source documents
risks.description: LLM generates plausible but factually incorrect responses not grounded in retrieved context, damaging trust in voice agents.
risks.mitigation: Vex faithfulness verification ensuring response stays within retrieved document scope.

risks.name: Latency budget violations
risks.description: Retrieval + LLM inference exceeds 300ms, causing unnatural conversation pauses in real-time voice interactions.
risks.mitigation: Vex performance guards with strict timeout enforcement and fallback responses.

risks.name: Chunk boundary errors losing critical context
risks.description: Document chunking splits key information across boundaries, causing incomplete or misleading retrievals.
risks.mitigation: Vex context completeness checks and metadata enrichment for boundary-aware retrieval.

implementation.title: LlamaIndex VectorStoreIndex Setup
implementation.description: Create a production-ready index from your knowledge base with optimal chunking for voice agent retrieval.
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex, SimpleDirectoryReader, Settings
from llama_index.embeddings.openai import OpenAIEmbedding

# Configure embeddings
Settings.embed_model = OpenAIEmbedding(model="text-embedding-3-small")

# Load documents
documents = SimpleDirectoryReader("data/").load_data()

# Create index with voice-optimized chunk size
index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(
    documents,
    chunk_size=512,  # Smaller chunks for voice latency
    chunk_overlap=50
)

query_engine = index.as_query_engine(
    similarity_top_k=3,
    streaming=True  # Enable streaming for low latency
)

implementation.title: Real-time STT to LlamaIndex Pipeline
implementation.description: Connect Deepgram STT to LlamaIndex query engine with conversation memory.
implementation.code:
import asyncio
from deepgram import DeepgramClient, PrerecordedOptions
from llama_index.core.memory import ChatMemoryBuffer

async def voice_pipeline(audio_data, query_engine):
    # STT
    deepgram = DeepgramClient("YOUR_DEEPGRAM_KEY")
    options = PrerecordedOptions(
        model="nova-2",
        smart_format=True,
        language="en"
    )
    response = await deepgram.listen.prerecorded.do(audio_data, options)
    query = response.results.channels[0].alternatives[0].transcript
    
    # Query with memory
    memory = ChatMemoryBuffer.from_defaults(token_limit=2000)
    response = await query_engine.aquery(query, memory=memory)
    
    return response.response

vexIntegration.title: Wrap LlamaIndex Query Engine with Vex
vexIntegration.description: Protect voice responses with comprehensive Vex verification including latency and safety.

vexIntegration.title: TypeScript Vex Verification Middleware
vexIntegration.description: Express middleware for voice API endpoints with Vex guards.

vexIntegration.title: Real-time Latency Budget Enforcement
vexIntegration.description: Vex monitors end-to-end voice pipeline latency with automatic fallbacks.
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import verify
import time

@verify(
    guards=[
        "latency.stt < 200ms",
        "latency.retrieval < 100ms",
        "latency.llm < 150ms",
        "latency.verification < 50ms"
    ],
    fallback="One moment while I process that..."
)
async def full_voice_pipeline(audio: bytes, query_engine):
    stt_start = time.time()
    transcript = await stt(audio)  # Deepgram
    stt_latency = time.time() - stt_start
    
    retrieval_start = time.time()
    response = str(query_engine.query(transcript))
    retrieval_latency = time.time() - retrieval_start
    
    return response
